channel.title: Worklog on Internship Report
channel.description: Recent content in Worklog on Internship Report

item.title: Worklog - Week 1
item.description:
Week 1 Objectives: Cloud Foundations & Reporting Tools

  • Duration: from 17/04/2026 to 23/04/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding Cloud Foundations & Reporting Tools.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Attend internship orientation, meet Mentors, and align team roles. 17/04/2026 17/04/2026 FCJ Internship Guidelines
2 Study Cloud Computing theory, differentiate service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) and benefits of cloud migration. 18/04/2026 18/04/2026 AWS Cloud Practitioner Essentials
3 Create AWS Free Tier account. Apply security best practices: enable MFA for Root, create IAM Admin User. 19/04/2026 20/04/2026 AWS IAM User Guide
4 Install Hugo Static Site Generator locally, run the template reporting website. 21/04/2026 21/04/2026 Hugo Docs
5 Get familiar with AWS Management Console navigation and complete 5 basic console labs. 22/04/2026 22/04/2026 AWS Console Guide
6 Read about AWS Billing management and set up basic budget alerts to control account spending. 23/04/2026 23/04/2026 AWS Billing Docs

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Understand the overall cloud computing landscape and the AWS ecosystem.
  • Complete basic account security configurations to prevent credential leaks or cost anomalies.
  • Successfully run the Hugo reporting website on localhost.

item.title: Worklog - Week 2
item.description:
Week 2 Objectives: Access Control & Authorization with AWS IAM

  • Duration: from 24/04/2026 to 30/04/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding Access Control & Authorization with AWS IAM.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Study AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) mechanics and Policy evaluation logic. 24/04/2026 25/04/2026 IAM Policies Overview
2 Practice creating Customer Managed Policies using JSON, applying the Principle of Least Privilege. 26/04/2026 27/04/2026 IAM JSON Policy Reference
3 Learn IAM Roles and temporary credential mechanisms for AWS resources. 28/04/2026 28/04/2026 IAM Roles Docs
4 Translate the first technical AWS Blog post related to identity management and access control security. 29/04/2026 30/04/2026 AWS Blogs

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Master writing custom IAM policies using JSON syntax.
  • Understand the differences between IAM Users, Groups, and IAM Roles.
  • Complete the first high-quality technical translation for blog publication.

item.title: Worklog - Week 3
item.description:
Week 3 Objectives: VPC Network Infrastructure Basics

  • Duration: from 01/05/2026 to 08/05/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding VPC Network Infrastructure Basics.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Study Amazon VPC components: CIDR blocks, Subnetting, Route Tables, Internet Gateways (IGW). 01/05/2026 03/05/2026 Amazon VPC User Guide
2 Design IP subnetting layout for Pet Shop project: 2 Public and 2 Private Subnets across 2 Availability Zones. 04/05/2026 05/05/2026 VPC CIDR Calculation
3 Create VPC on AWS Console, provision Subnets and attach Internet Gateway. 06/05/2026 07/05/2026 VPC Console Hands-on
4 Configure Route Tables: Associate Public Subnets with Route Table pointing to IGW. 08/05/2026 08/05/2026 VPC Route Tables Docs

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Gain deep understanding of IP subnetting calculations.
  • Independently design and deploy a custom VPC with internet connectivity.
  • Clearly define the boundary between Public and Private subnets.

item.title: Worklog - Week 4
item.description:
Week 4 Objectives: Advanced Routing & Traffic Flow Control

  • Duration: from 09/05/2026 to 17/05/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding Advanced Routing & Traffic Flow Control.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Study NAT Gateway. Create a NAT Gateway in the Public Subnet and configure routing for Private Route Tables. 09/05/2026 11/05/2026 NAT Gateway Documentation
2 Compare NAT Gateway with NAT Instance on scalability, management, and real cost optimization. 12/05/2026 13/05/2026 VPC NAT Comparison
3 Learn VPC Endpoints. Create a Gateway Endpoint to allow private EC2 instance to access S3 directly. 14/05/2026 15/05/2026 VPC Endpoints Docs
4 Study network security controls: Differentiate Security Groups (Stateful) and Network ACLs (Stateless). 16/05/2026 17/05/2026 VPC Security Docs

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Configure outbound Internet routing for Private Subnet via secure NAT Gateway.
  • Optimize data transfer costs using S3 Gateway Endpoint.
  • Master configuring Security Groups to secure instances.

item.title: Worklog - Week 5
item.description:
Week 5 Objectives: Cloud Storage with Amazon S3

  • Duration: from 18/05/2026 to 24/05/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding Cloud Storage with Amazon S3.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Study Amazon S3, Buckets, Objects, Permissions, and S3 Storage Classes. 18/05/2026 19/05/2026 Amazon S3 Developer Guide
2 Create S3 Bucket, configure Static Website Hosting to prepare for ReactJS Frontend hosting. 20/05/2026 21/05/2026 S3 Static Website Hosting
3 Configure S3 CORS Policies to allow cross-origin requests. Apply public read S3 Bucket Policy. 22/05/2026 22/05/2026 S3 CORS & Policies Docs
4 Study S3 Lifecycle Rules, configure automatic archiving rules to S3 Glacier to minimize storage cost. 23/05/2026 24/05/2026 S3 Lifecycle Management

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Learn how to host a static website on Amazon S3.
  • Secure S3 bucket access using CORS and Bucket Policies.
  • Optimize storage costs by configuring S3 Lifecycle Rules.

item.title: Worklog - Week 6
item.description:
Week 6 Objectives: CDN Distribution & Edge Protection with WAF

  • Duration: from 25/05/2026 to 01/06/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding CDN Distribution & Edge Protection with WAF.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Study CDN service Amazon CloudFront. Create a CloudFront Distribution with S3 as the origin. 25/05/2026 27/05/2026 Amazon CloudFront Guide
2 Configure Origin Access Control (OAC) to restrict direct S3 bucket access and enforce CDN-only routing. 28/05/2026 29/05/2026 CloudFront OAC Docs
3 Study AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall). Create a Web ACL to block SQL injection and Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) attacks. 30/05/2026 31/05/2026 AWS WAF Developer Guide
4 Associate WAF Web ACL with CloudFront Distribution to secure edge traffic flow. 01/06/2026 01/06/2026 WAF CloudFront Integration

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Accelerate static site loading times globally using CloudFront CDN.
  • Complete origin protection for S3 using CloudFront OAC.
  • Enforce security rules against common web vulnerabilities via AWS WAF.

item.title: Worklog - Week 7
item.description:
Week 7 Objectives: Virtual Servers with Amazon EC2

  • Duration: from 02/06/2026 to 09/06/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding Virtual Servers with Amazon EC2.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Learn Amazon EC2. Compare Instance Types and study EBS volume storage. 02/06/2026 03/06/2026 Amazon EC2 User Guide
2 Provision EC2 Instance running Amazon Linux 2023 placed inside the Private Subnet. 04/06/2026 05/06/2026 EC2 Console Hands-on
3 Configure Security Group SG_Backend restricting direct internet access, opening only port 8080 to the ALB. 06/06/2026 07/06/2026 EC2 Security Groups Docs
4 Set up application environment: Connect via Systems Manager Session Manager, install Amazon Corretto 17. 08/06/2026 09/06/2026 AWS Systems Manager User Guide

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Understand EC2 instance lifecycle, types, and storage.
  • Deploy secure EC2 instance in a Private Subnet without public IP addresses.
  • Eliminate SSH port 22 access by leveraging AWS Systems Manager Session Manager.

item.title: Worklog - Week 8
item.description:
Week 8 Objectives: Load Balancing & Auto Scaling

  • Duration: from 10/06/2026 to 17/06/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding Load Balancing & Auto Scaling.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Study Application Load Balancer (ALB). Create a Target Group for port 8080 and provision ALB in Public Subnets. 10/06/2026 11/06/2026 ALB Developer Guide
2 Research EC2 Auto Scaling. Create a Launch Template storing instance type, custom AMI, and User Data script. 12/06/2026 14/06/2026 Launch Templates Docs
3 Provision Auto Scaling Group (ASG) integrated with the ALB Target Group. 15/06/2026 16/06/2026 Auto Scaling Groups Guide
4 Configure Target Tracking Scaling Policy on CPUUtilization: Desired 2, Min 2, Max 4. 17/06/2026 17/06/2026 ASG Dynamic Scaling Policies

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Understand ALB request routing and distribution.
  • Configure highly resilient Auto Scaling Group architectures.
  • Use EC2 User Data to bootstrap Spring Boot applications automatically on scaling events.

item.title: Worklog - Week 9
item.description:
Week 9 Objectives: Relational Databases & Cache Clusters

  • Duration: from 18/06/2026 to 25/06/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding Relational Databases & Cache Clusters.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Study Amazon RDS. Compare Single-AZ vs Multi-AZ deployment models. 18/06/2026 19/06/2026 Amazon RDS User Guide
2 Provision a MySQL Database Instance running in Multi-AZ (Primary/Standby) on a Private Subnet Group. 20/06/2026 21/06/2026 RDS Multi-AZ Deployment
3 Configure Database Security Groups to allow port 3306 connection only from SG_Backend EC2 instances. 22/06/2026 23/06/2026 RDS Security Groups Docs
4 Learn Amazon ElastiCache. Launch a Redis cluster inside Private Subnets to store cached data for the Pet Shop. 24/06/2026 25/06/2026 Amazon ElastiCache Docs

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Deploy highly available RDS Multi-AZ database engines.
  • Apply zero trust network isolation to databases using Security Groups.
  • Integrate Redis caching to improve read performance.

item.title: Worklog - Week 10
item.description:
Week 10 Objectives: Monitoring, Alerting & Security Configuration

  • Duration: from 26/06/2026 to 02/07/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding Monitoring, Alerting & Security Configuration.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Study AWS KMS (Key Management Service). Create Customer Managed Keys to encrypt EBS volumes. 26/06/2026 27/06/2026 AWS KMS Developer Guide
2 Store Database credentials and integration secret tokens securely inside AWS Secrets Manager. 28/06/2026 28/06/2026 AWS Secrets Manager Guide
3 Configure Amazon CloudWatch. Create custom Dashboards tracking RAM, EC2 CPU, and ALB Request Count. 29/06/2026 30/06/2026 CloudWatch Metrics Guide
4 Create CloudWatch Alarms and integrate with Amazon SNS to send email notifications when CPU > 80%. 01/07/2026 02/07/2026 CloudWatch Alarm with SNS

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Learn to encrypt data at rest and protect sensitive configuration.
  • Set up real-time monitoring dashboards.
  • Automate incident alerting on resource overload.

item.title: Worklog - Week 11
item.description:
Week 11 Objectives: Architecture Design & Data Flow Mapping

  • Duration: from 03/07/2026 to 10/07/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding Architecture Design & Data Flow Mapping.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Synthesize AWS services learned in Phase 1. Draft a highly available 3-tier architecture design for Pet Resort & Care. 03/07/2026 05/07/2026 AWS Architecture Center
2 Use Draw.io to draw the detailed system architecture diagram, showing subnets, Security Groups, and IAM Roles. 06/07/2026 07/07/2026 Draw.io AWS 3D Template
3 Map out detailed data flow diagrams and connectivity rules (Frontend -> CloudFront -> ALB -> EC2 -> RDS/Redis). 08/07/2026 09/07/2026 Data Flow Design
4 Complete the Project Proposal document, estimating monthly infrastructure costs via AWS Pricing Calculator. 10/07/2026 10/07/2026 AWS Pricing Calculator

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Complete a professional system architecture diagram following AWS patterns.
  • Define clean network communication paths between subnets.
  • Estimate cloud spending budgets optimized for the project.

item.title: Worklog - Week 12
item.description:
Week 12 Objectives: Multi-tier System Cloud Deployment

  • Duration: from 11/07/2026 to 20/07/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding Multi-tier System Cloud Deployment.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Provision core network: Create VPC, Subnets, Internet/NAT Gateways, Route Tables, and Security Groups. 11/07/2026 12/07/2026 Infrastructure-as-Code Basics
2 Deploy Frontend: Build ReactJS static bundle, host on S3, configure CloudFront CDN and attach WAF Web ACL. 13/07/2026 14/07/2026 S3 & CloudFront Deployment
3 Provision RDS MySQL Multi-AZ and ElastiCache Redis cluster within Private Subnets. 15/07/2026 16/07/2026 RDS & Redis Provisioning
4 Deploy Backend: Launch base EC2, configure Spring Boot application environment, integrate Database and Cache. 17/07/2026 17/07/2026 Spring Boot EC2 Setup
5 Create custom AMI from base EC2. Create Launch Template referencing the AMI and deploy Auto Scaling Group. 18/07/2026 19/07/2026 AWS EC2 AMI Creation
6 Set up Application Load Balancer pointing to ASG. Adjust CORS configs and test end-to-end integration. 20/07/2026 20/07/2026 ALB Routing Setup

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Successfully host the secure multi-tier full-stack application on AWS Cloud.
  • Automate compute instances deployment using custom AMI and Launch Templates.
  • Integrate Frontend-Backend secure communication over HTTPS.

item.title: Worklog - Week 13
item.description:
Week 13 Objectives: QA Load Testing, Security Hardening & Project Release

  • Duration: from 21/07/2026 to 30/07/2026.
  • Core focus: Learning and accomplishing tasks regarding QA Load Testing, Security Hardening & Project Release.
Day Work & Tasks Accomplished Start Date End Date References
1 Perform functional QA testing of booking and cart actions on the deployed Frontend. 21/07/2026 22/07/2026 Functional Testing Tools
2 Write load testing scenarios using Artillery. Simulate 100 concurrent users hitting the backend API endpoints. 23/07/2026 24/07/2026 Artillery Load Testing Guide
3 Analyze CloudWatch metrics, resolve DB Connection Pool limits and 504 Gateway Timeout errors by tuning Spring Boot HikariCP pool and ALB timeouts. 25/07/2026 26/07/2026 CloudWatch Performance Tuning
4 Practice FinOps: Audit real cloud spend, scale down overprovisioned RDS/EC2 resources, and clean up orphan resources. 27/07/2026 28/07/2026 AWS Cost Optimization Practices
5 Complete and review the final 13-week internship report. Package code repositories and deliver the final release production build. 29/07/2026 30/07/2026 Project Final Release Docs

Key Accomplishments of the Week:

  • Validate system scalability and load tolerance under load.
  • Optimize AWS billing (FinOps) to avoid unnecessary budget overflow.
  • Deliver a complete, highly structured final internship report.
